strengths: 20+ years of polyglot production engineering across web, mobile, embedded systems, high-throughput microservices, and cloud infrastructure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) — a breadth that far exceeds the 15-year floor and signals the cross-domain technical authority this director-level IC role demands.

Actively building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and frameworks at re:cinq today, demonstrating hands-on, production-level insight into agentic workflows — the precise AI platform depth the role prizes in a preferred candidate.

Proven founder, community builder, and public educator: nine-year run founding and shipping GISCollective, a top-10 US Apple TV app at WatchUp, and a sustained record of conference talks (FOSS4G) and multi-day workshops establish the ability to inspire, teach, and deliver end-to-end — a direct match for the role's 'inspire others internally and externally' responsibility.
concerns: No advanced degree in an AI-related field; highest credential is a BS in Computer Science plus a Fine Arts bachelor's — the preferred Master's or PhD is absent.

Strategic advisory and partner-facing scope (advising enterprise customers, shaping cloud product roadmaps at a director level) would be a meaningful expansion from the candidate's primarily hands-on engineering background.
Concentrated AI platform depth is recent (April 2025–present); a longer established track record of AI-specific product delivery at scale would further strengthen the candidacy.

key_factors.factor: Breadth and tenure of engineering experience
Active hands-on AI and MCP platform building
Founder, community educator, and public speaker track record

response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings over two decades of polyglot, production-grade engineering spanning web, mobile, embedded systems, high-throughput microservices, and cloud infrastructure — a rare cross-domain fluency that maps directly to the technical authority this role requires. His current work at re:cinq, building MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks, places him at the active frontier of AI platform engineering rather than observing it from the outside. Coupled with a nine-year founder run on GISCollective, a top-10 ranked consumer app, and a sustained record of public teaching through conference talks and multi-day workshops, Szabo demonstrates the builder-educator-leader combination the Principal Engineer (AI Strategy) role explicitly prizes — making him a compelling candidate whose hands-on AI momentum and outsized technical range argue strongly for advancement.
